content: The language learning vocabulary acquisition process (rooted in the lexical sense of reflexion as reflection on meaning) can be restructured using the iterative self-feedback framework of LLM Self-Refine to boost long-term retention and contextual usage mastery. Learners first generate an initial guess of a new word's meaning from context, then iteratively self-reflect on mismatches between their guess and actual usage, refining their mental definition step-by-step instead of memorizing a dictionary definition upfront.
